questions.question: Calculate the Information Gain for the 'Company' attribute using the provided concert dataset.
expectedPoints: Calculate initial entropy of the dataset.
Calculate entropy for each value of 'Company' (Friends, Alone).
Compute the weighted average entropy after the split.
Subtract the weighted average from the initial entropy to get IG.

questions.question: Compare and contrast the ID3 and CART algorithms. Discuss their splitting criteria, handling of data, and approach to avoiding overfitting.
expectedPoints: ID3 uses Information Gain; CART uses Gini for classification.
ID3 handles only categorical data; CART handles both numeric and categorical.
ID3 creates multi-way trees; CART creates strict binary trees.
ID3 is prone to overfitting; CART uses cost-complexity pruning.
Mention that CART can be used for regression, while ID3 cannot.
